title: 10 Herbal Teas for Altitude Sickness and Other High-Altitude Ills
intro_desc: Herbal teas for altitude sickness are a natural remedy that helps alleviate symptoms associated with high altitudes, such as headaches, fatigue, and dizziness. These teas are effective in reducing altitude sickness due to their adaptogenic properties, which enable the body to adapt to changes in oxygen levels. The best herbal teas for altitude sickness include Ginkgo biloba, Astragalus membranaceus, Rhodiola rosea, Zingiber officinale, Andrographis paniculata, Panax ginseng, Withania somnifera, Cinchona officinalis, Echinacea purpurea, and Glycyrrhiza glabra. If left untreated, altitude sickness can lead to severe consequences, including respiratory failure, brain damage, and even death, significantly impacting one's quality of life and daily activities.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ginkgo biloba tea has been used to help people who travel to high places and feel sick. This sickness is called altitude sickness or mountain sickness. It happens when the air gets thinner and the body doesn't get enough oxygen. Ginkgo biloba tea can help because it improves blood flow and increases oxygen delivery to the body's cells. This is good for people who are experiencing altitude sickness, as it can help reduce the symptoms such as headache, nausea, and fatigue. Ginkgo biloba also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the brain and other parts of the body that can be caused by altitude sickness. The tea is made from the leaves of the Ginkgo biloba tree, which has been used for centuries in traditional medicine to improve blood flow and oxygen delivery. Drinking Ginkgo biloba tea may help people feel better when they are at high altitudes and can be used in addition to other treatments for altitude sickness.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for altitude sickness because it helps to improve circulation to the brain and reduce inflammation in blood vessels, which can get damaged by the lower air pressure at high altitudes.

Ginkgolide B is good for altitude sickness because it has been found to improve blood flow and reduce inflammation in the body, which helps to alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue.

Isorhapontigenin is good for altitude sickness because it helps to reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can cause symptoms such as headaches and fatigue that are associated with high altitude.
Bilobalide is good for altitude sickness because it helps improve blood circulation to the brain and reduces inflammation, which can reduce the severity of altitude sickness symptoms.
Kaempferol is good for altitude sickness because it helps reduce inflammation and relax blood vessels, which can help the body adjust to the lower air pressure at high altitudes.

remedies.image_alt: Ginkgo biloba herbal teas for altitude sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Andrographis paniculata herbal tea that helps with altitude sickness, start by gathering the right ingredients: fresh or dried Andrographis paniculata leaves, a tea infuser, and a pot. Choose a quiet and comfortable place to make the tea. First, measure one teaspoon of dried leaves or two teaspoons of fresh leaves for every eight ounces of water. Place the leaves in the tea infuser and set it in the pot. Next, boil fresh water and carefully pour it over the leaves in the infuser. Allow the mixture to steep for five to seven minutes, depending on your preference for the strength of the tea. After the steeping time has passed, carefully remove the infuser from the pot and discard the leaves. Finally, let the tea cool down and enjoy it hot or iced, ideally taking one to two cups per day to help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Astragalus membranaceus tea is a traditional Chinese medicine that has been used for centuries to help people adapt to high altitudes. At high elevations, the air is thinner and there's less oxygen, which can lead to altitude sickness. Astragalus membranaceus tea works by increasing the production of red blood cells, which carry oxygen to the body's tissues. This helps to reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches, fatigue, and dizziness. The tea also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the brain and other tissues caused by altitude sickness. Additionally, Astragalus membranaceus tea helps to improve circulation and increase oxygen delivery to the body's tissues, making it easier to breathe and move around at high altitudes. Some studies have shown that drinking Astragalus membranaceus tea can reduce the severity and duration of altitude sickness, allowing people to climb higher and stay at high elevations for longer periods.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Flavonoids are good for altitude sickness because they help to increase blood flow and oxygen supply to the body, reducing the risk of high-altitude illnesses by lowering blood pressure and inflammation.
Saponins are good for altitude sickness because they help to reduce inflammation and increase oxygen supply in the body, making it easier for people to adapt to high altitudes.

Polysaccharides are good for altitude sickness because they help to reduce inflammation, improve oxygen delivery to the body, and boost the immune system, making it easier for people to adapt to high altitudes.

remedies.image_alt: Astragalus membranaceus herbal teas for altitude sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Andrographis paniculata herbal tea to help with altitude sickness, first, you need to gather the necessary ingredients, which include dried Andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers, and a pot to boil water in. Next, add a teaspoon of the dried herbs to a cup, and pour boiling water over the herbs. Let it steep for 5 to 7 minutes, allowing the active compounds to infuse into the water. After the steeping time is up, strain the tea into another cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure not to add any sugar as it can slow down the digestion process. Andrographis paniculata tea is traditionally consumed 3 to 4 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as nausea and headaches, and to boost overall immune function.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale tea, also known as ginger tea, has been used for centuries to help people feel better when they are in high places. When you go to high altitudes, your body has trouble getting enough oxygen. This can cause headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Ginger tea can help with this problem. The active compounds in ginger, called gingerols and shogaols, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling and pain in the body. Ginger tea can also help to reduce nausea and improve digestion, which can be a big help when you're feeling unwell at high altitude. Additionally, ginger has natural antioxidant properties that can help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals, which can be formed when your body is under stress from high altitude. Drinking ginger tea may help to alleviate some of the symptoms of altitude sickness, making it a helpful remedy for people who live in or travel to high-altitude areas.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Gingerols are good for altitude sickness because they help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help to alleviate the symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and dizziness.

Shogaols are good for altitude sickness because they help to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help to alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue often experienced at high elevations.
Zingiberene helps alleviate altitude sickness by acting as an anti-inflammatory, which reduces swelling in the brain caused by high altitudes and promotes better blood flow to the brain.

remedies.image_alt: Zingiber officinale herbal teas for altitude sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Andrographis paniculata herbal tea that helps with altitude sickness, start by gathering the necessary ingredients: dried Andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers, a large pot, a strainer, and a cup. Begin by boiling one liter of water in the large pot. Once the water starts boiling, add one teaspoon of dried Andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers for every 250ml of water. Reduce the heat to low and let the mixture simmer for 5-7 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse into the water. After the infusion time is complete, carefully remove the pot from the heat source and let it sit for a minute or two. Next, use the strainer to strain the tea into a cup, pressing on the solids to extract as much liquid as possible. Discard the solids and your Andrographis paniculata tea is ready to drink. It's recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a day when traveling to high altitudes to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Echinacea purpurea tea is a natural remedy that may help with altitude sickness. This plant has been used for a long time to boost the immune system, which is often weakened at high elevations. When you drink Echinacea purpurea tea, it may help increase oxygen levels in your body and reduce inflammation caused by high altitude. It also has antioxidant properties that can protect your body from damage caused by free radicals. Additionally, Echinacea purpurea tea may help reduce fatigue and stress, which are common symptoms of altitude sickness. Some studies suggest that Echinacea purpurea may also help regulate blood pressure and heart rate, which can be affected by high altitude. Drinking Echinacea purpurea tea may also help your body adapt to the lower oxygen levels at high elevations, making it a popular choice among hikers and travelers who spend time at high altitudes.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quercetin is good for altitude sickness because it helps to reduce inflammation and improve oxygen delivery to the body's cells, making it easier to adapt to high altitudes.

Cichoric acid is good for altitude sickness because it helps reduce inflammation in the body and improves blood flow to the lungs, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and shortness of breath.
Tannins are good for altitude sickness because they help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the lungs, which helps the body adapt to high altitudes more easily.
Caffeic acid is good for altitude sickness because it helps reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the body, which can cause headaches and other symptoms associated with high altitude.
Alkylamides are good for altitude sickness because they have been shown to help prevent and reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness by improving blood flow and reducing inflammation in the body.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Andrographis paniculata herbal tea for altitude sickness, start by gathering dried Andrographis paniculata leaves. You can purchase these leaves from a health food store or online. Next, boil a cup of water and let it cool for a few minutes. Add one teaspoon of dried Andrographis paniculata leaves to the cooled water, and let it steep for 5-7 minutes. The longer it steeps, the stronger the tea will be. Strain the tea into a cup to remove the leaves, and add honey or lemon to taste if desired. It's recommended to drink this tea 2-3 times a day for relief from altitude sickness symptoms such as headaches and nausea. You can also combine Andrographis paniculata with other herbs like ginseng or ginger to enhance its benefits. Always consult with a healthcare professional before using herbal remedies, especially if you're experiencing severe altitude sickness symptoms.

remedies.remedy_desc: Panax ginseng tea is a traditional remedy that may help people who travel to high altitudes. At high elevations, the air has less oxygen, which can cause headaches, fatigue, and nausea. Panax ginseng tea is believed to help the body adapt to this lower oxygen environment. It is thought to improve blood flow and increase the amount of oxygen delivered to the body's cells. This can help reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue. Panax ginseng tea also contains antioxidants, which help protect the body from damage caused by free radicals. Some people believe that drinking Panax ginseng tea before traveling to a high altitude can help prevent altitude sickness from developing in the first place.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Polyphenols are good for altitude sickness because they help reduce inflammation, improve oxygen delivery to the body, and increase blood flow to the brain, which can alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue.
Ginsenoside Rg1 may be good for altitude sickness because it helps lower blood pressure and improves blood flow to the brain, making it easier for the body to adapt to high altitudes.

Ginsenoside Rb1 is good for altitude sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the brain caused by high altitude, which can lead to a decrease in oxygen delivery to the body's tissues.

remedies.image_alt: Panax ginseng herbal teas for altitude sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Andrographis paniculata herbal tea that can help with altitude sickness, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Andrographis paniculata leaves, which can be purchased at a local health food store or online. Next, boil one cup of water and then add one teaspoon of the dried leaves. Allow the mixture to steep for 5-10 minutes to release the active compounds from the plant. Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the solids. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but be sure to drink the tea hot as this helps to release the beneficial properties of the plant. Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, preferably in the morning and evening, to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches and fatigue. It's also essential to note that while Andrographis paniculata tea may help alleviate symptoms, it's still crucial to ascend to high altitudes gradually and follow proper acclimatization techniques to avoid more severe forms of altitude sickness.

remedies.remedy_desc: Cinchona officinalis tea, also known as Peruvian bark tea, is a natural remedy for altitude sickness. At high elevations, the air has less oxygen, and our bodies have trouble getting enough oxygen. Cinchona officinalis contains a special compound called quinine, which helps our blood vessels dilate or expand. This allows more oxygen-rich blood to flow to our brain and other vital organs. As a result, our bodies can adapt to the lower oxygen levels more easily. Additionally, Cinchona officinalis tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce swelling in the brain caused by altitude sickness. It also has a diuretic effect, which can help our body get rid of excess fluids that can accumulate at high elevations.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Quinine is good for altitude sickness because it helps to reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue caused by high altitudes.
Quinidine is good for altitude sickness because it helps prevent a drop in blood oxygen levels by opening up airways and reducing inflammation in the lungs.
Quinic acid is good for altitude sickness because it helps reduce the level of inflammatory chemicals in the body, making it easier for people to adapt to high altitudes.
Tannins are good for altitude sickness because they help increase oxygen supply to the body, reduce inflammation and swelling in the lungs, and prevent blood clots that can worsen the condition.
Flavonoids are good for altitude sickness because they help to thin out blood, which improves oxygen supply to the body's organs and reduces the risk of complications.

Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain the liquid into a cup using a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve. Discard the solids and add honey or lemon to taste, if desired. Once prepared, Andrographis paniculata tea can be consumed 2-3 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and fatigue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Ephedra sinica tea has been used for centuries in traditional Chinese medicine to help with altitude sickness. This is because Ephedra sinica contains a compound called ephedrine, which helps to improve blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body's cells. When we travel to high altitudes, our bodies have to work harder to get enough oxygen. Ephedrine in Ephedra sinica tea can help make this process easier by widening blood vessels and reducing blood pressure. This allows more oxygen to reach our muscles and brain, which can help prevent altitude sickness symptoms like headaches, fatigue, and nausea. Additionally, Ephedra sinica tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can also help to reduce swelling and discomfort associated with altitude sickness. By drinking Ephedra sinica tea, people can help their bodies adapt to high altitudes more easily and feel better when traveling to high elevations.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Ephedrine is good for altitude sickness because it helps to increase oxygen levels in the blood and reduce the heart rate, making it easier for the body to adapt to high altitudes.
Tannins are good for altitude sickness because they have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce swelling in the lungs and brain caused by high altitude.

Polyphenols are good for altitude sickness because they help to reduce inflammation, improve blood flow to the brain, and prevent damage to cells caused by high altitudes, thereby reducing the risk of headaches, nausea, and fatigue associated with altitude sickness.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Andrographis paniculata herbal tea that helps with altitude sickness, start by gathering dried Andrographis paniculata leaves or roots. Use a tablespoon of dried leaves or a teaspoon of dried roots for every 8 ounces of water. Boil fresh water in a teapot and add the dried Andrographis paniculata, then let it steep for 5-7 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves or roots. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's best to drink the tea plain to get the full benefits. Drink 2-3 cups of Andrographis paniculata tea throughout the day, especially before and after ascending to high altitudes, as it is believed to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium tea is a natural remedy that can help with altitude sickness. This tea is made from the leaves of the wormwood plant, which has been used for centuries to treat various health issues. At high altitudes, the air pressure is lower, making it harder for the body to get the oxygen it needs. This can cause headaches, fatigue, and nausea. Artemisia absinthium tea contains compounds called sesquiterpenes, which can help to relax blood vessels and improve blood flow to the brain. This can help to reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and dizziness. Additionally, the tea has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling in the brain and other tissues. Some people also drink Artemisia absinthium tea to help with its antioxidant properties, which can help to protect the body from damage caused by free radicals.

Thujone is good for altitude sickness because it has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling in the brain and improve blood flow to the lungs, making it easier for the body to adapt to high altitudes.

Tannins are good for altitude sickness because they help improve circulation and reduce inflammation in the body, which can ease the symptoms of altitude sickness like headaches and shortness of breath.

Polyphenols are good for altitude sickness because they help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the body's cells, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue often associated with high altitudes.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Andrographis paniculata herbal tea that helps with altitude sickness, start by gathering the necessary ingredients. You will need dried Andrographis paniculata leaves, which can be found in health food stores or online. To prepare the tea, boil 1 cup of water and then add 1 teaspoon of dried Andrographis paniculata leaves. Let it steep for 5-7 minutes to allow the herbs to infuse into the water. Strain the tea into a cup and discard the leaves. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's recommended to drink it plain for the best results. It's also essential to note that Andrographis paniculata tea is not a substitute for medical treatment, and if you are experiencing severe altitude sickness symptoms, you should seek medical attention immediately. However, this herbal tea can be a helpful supplement to help alleviate mild symptoms such as headaches and nausea. Drink 2-3 cups of the tea per day, preferably 1 hour before ascending to high altitudes.

remedies.remedy_desc: Rhodiola rosea tea is a natural remedy that can help with altitude sickness. At high elevations, the air is thinner, and our bodies need more oxygen. This can cause headaches, fatigue, and nausea. Rhodiola rosea is an herb that helps our bodies adapt to high altitudes by increasing the production of red blood cells. Red blood cells carry oxygen to our muscles and organs. By increasing their production, Rhodiola rosea tea helps to reduce the symptoms of altitude sickness. It also has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce swelling and pain in the head and body. Additionally, Rhodiola rosea tea can help to improve mental performance and reduce stress, which can be a common problem at high elevations. By drinking Rhodiola rosea tea, people can help to reduce their symptoms and feel more comfortable at high altitudes.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Salidroside is good for altitude sickness because it helps to increase red blood cell count and reduce oxidative stress in the body, which can help alleviate symptoms like fatigue and shortness of breath associated with high altitudes.
Rosavin is good for altitude sickness because it helps to thin blood and improve circulation, which can reduce the risk of headaches and other symptoms associated with high altitudes.

Polyphenols are good for altitude sickness because they help reduce inflammation and improve blood flow to the brain, which can help alleviate symptoms such as headaches and dizziness that occur when the body is not used to high altitudes.

remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Andrographis paniculata herbal tea that helps with altitude sickness, start by gathering fresh Andrographis paniculata leaves and flowers, or use dried ones if you can't find fresh ones. If using fresh leaves, rinse them gently with water, and then chop them into small pieces. If using dried leaves, you can skip this step. Next, boil a cup of water in a pot, then add one teaspoon of chopped Andrographis leaves or one teaspoon of dried leaves to the boiling water. Reduce the heat to low and let it simmer for 5-7 minutes. Remove the pot from the heat and let the tea steep for another 2-3 minutes. Strain the tea into a cup using a tea strainer or a piece of cheesecloth to remove the leaves. Your tea is now ready to drink. You can add honey or lemon to taste, but it's not necessary. Drink the tea 2-3 times a day to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches and nausea.

remedies.remedy_desc: Andrographis paniculata tea is a traditional remedy that may help alleviate altitude sickness symptoms. At high elevations, the body struggles to get enough oxygen, causing headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Andrographis paniculata contains a compound called andrographolide, which has anti-inflammatory properties that may help reduce swelling in the body. This can make it easier to breathe at high altitudes. The tea also contains antioxidants that may help protect the body from oxidative stress, which can occur when the body is not getting enough oxygen. Drinking Andrographis paniculata tea may also help to regulate the body's natural response to high altitude, which can lead to a decrease in symptoms such as headaches and nausea. The tea is also believed to boost the immune system, which can help the body to adapt to high altitudes more easily.

remedy_constituents.constituent_desc: Andrographolide is good for altitude sickness because it helps reduce inflammation and improves blood flow to the brain, which can alleviate symptoms such as headaches and fatigue caused by high altitudes.
Quercetin is good for altitude sickness because it helps reduce inflammation and improve oxygen delivery to the body's cells, making it easier for people to adapt to high altitudes.
Tannins are good for altitude sickness because they have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce the swelling and damage in the brain caused by high altitudes.

remedies.image_alt: Andrographis paniculata herbal teas for altitude sickness
remedies.remedy_preparation: To make Andrographis paniculata herbal tea, start by gathering dried leaves and stems of the plant. You can buy these from a health food store or online. Rinse the dried herbs with a little water to remove any dirt, then place one teaspoon of the mixture into a tea infuser or a small muslin bag. Boil a cup of water, then carefully pour it over the herbs in the infuser or bag. Let the mixture steep for 5-7 minutes, allowing the flavors and benefits to seep into the water. Once steeped, carefully remove the infuser or bag, and strain the tea into a cup. Add honey or lemon to taste, but avoid adding sugar, as it can counteract the benefits of the tea. Andrographis paniculata herbal tea is traditionally used to help alleviate symptoms of altitude sickness, such as headaches, nausea, and fatigue, by improving blood flow and oxygen delivery to the body.

supplementary_best: A combination of Rhodiola rosea, Ginkgo biloba, and Cinchona officinalis herbal teas may help remedy altitude sickness. Rhodiola rosea is known to reduce stress and fatigue, which are common symptoms of altitude sickness. Ginkgo biloba improves blood circulation and oxygen delivery to the body's cells, which is crucial at high altitudes. Cinchona officinalis, also known as the source of quinine, helps increase red blood cell count and alleviate altitude sickness symptoms such as headaches and nausea. This combination addresses multiple aspects of altitude sickness, including stress and fatigue, oxygen delivery, and red blood cell count. Drinking a cup of Rhodiola rosea tea before ascending to high altitudes may help prepare the body, while Ginkgo biloba and Cinchona officinalis teas can be consumed throughout the stay to alleviate symptoms.
supplementary_avoid: If you have altitude sickness, you should avoid certain herbal teas that can make symptoms worse. One such tea is Yerba Mate, which can increase heart rate and blood pressure. This can be a problem at high altitudes where the air is thinner and the body already has a harder time getting enough oxygen.  Another tea to avoid is Guarana, which contains a stimulant called guaranine that can increase heart rate and blood pressure even more. This can lead to dehydration, headaches, and other complications associated with altitude sickness.  You should also be cautious with Ginseng varieties other than 'Panax ginseng', like 'Eleuthero' or 'Asian Ginseng', as they can cause blood vessels to constrict, which may worsen altitude sickness symptoms.  Additionally, some teas made from Ephedra's relatives like 'Mahuang' or 'Siberian Ginseng' contain similar stimulants that can increase blood pressure and heart rate, making symptoms worse.
supplementary_other_remedies: When traveling to high elevations, some people experience altitude sickness, which can cause headaches, nausea, and fatigue. Besides drinking herbal teas, other natural remedies can help alleviate these symptoms. Ginger has been used for centuries to calm the stomach and reduce nausea. Its anti-inflammatory properties can also help ease headaches. You can consume ginger in the form of fresh juice, dried powder, or cooked in soups and meals. Another effective remedy is cayenne pepper, which contains capsaicin that helps thin the blood and improve oxygen delivery to the body's tissues. Drinking water with lemon and honey can also help rehydrate the body and provide essential electrolytes. Additionally, some people find that inhaling oxygen-rich air from a portable oxygen tank or taking a warm bath can help ease the discomfort of altitude sickness. These remedies can provide relief by reducing the severity of symptoms and allowing the body to acclimate to the higher elevation.
supplementary_related_ailments: People with altitude sickness often experience other health issues. One common problem is dehydration. At high altitudes, the air is drier, and people lose more water through breathing. Medicinal herbs like dandelion root and licorice root can help with dehydration. Dandelion root increases urine production, helping the body get rid of excess fluids. Licorice root has anti-inflammatory properties that reduce swelling in the body, making it easier for the body to retain water.  Another issue is headaches. Altitude sickness can cause headaches due to changes in blood pressure and the lack of oxygen. Feverfew, a medicinal herb, can help with headaches. It contains compounds called sesquiterpene lactones that reduce inflammation and pain.  Some people with altitude sickness also experience nausea and vomiting. Ginger, a medicinal herb, can help with nausea. It has compounds called gingerols and shogaols that reduce nausea and inflammation in the stomach.  High altitudes can also cause fatigue and weakness. Ashwagandha, a medicinal herb, can help with this. It contains compounds called withanolides that reduce stress and increase energy levels.
